title: Golavo Tournament Retrospective
description: A per-match backtest of a finished tournament. Every row is a backtest computed at that match's own kickoff-1s cutoff — the same conservative information boundary the seal and the Match Cockpit replay use — so a row is exactly what the app would have said had it been asked that moment. Nothing here is a sealed record or a ledger artifact. Some matches carry a day-precision (00:00 UTC) kickoff stand-in rather than a verified time; when such a row shares its calendar day with a later match, it can train that later match's forecast even though it may really have been played afterwards. The module does not tighten its cutoff to hide this — the `exposure` envelope field and each row's `training_same_day_proxy_rows` disclose it. Derived from core/golavo_core/retrospective.py:world_cup_2026_retrospective (RETROSPECTIVE_SCHEMA_VERSION).

reason.description: Why no retrospective could be produced, e.g. no completed matches in this snapshot's tournament window. Present only when status is unavailable.

ranking_family.description: The single family biggest_surprises is ranked by — the app's own DEFAULT_FAMILY. A cross-family average would describe a forecast the app never makes.

families.description: Four voices, not five: bivariate_poisson is numerically identical to poisson_independent on every recorded fold, so it is not offered as a separate opinion.

exposure.description: How many backtested matches trained on at least one same-day, day-precision (00:00 UTC) proxy row that cannot be proven to have happened first. Disclosed, not hidden — the app's own kickoff-1s cutoff is never tightened to paper over this.

rows_with_same_day_proxies.description: Count of rows in matches whose training_same_day_proxy_rows is greater than zero.

biggest_surprises.description: The same rows as matches, ordered by ranking_family's log_loss, worst-surprise first.

kickoff_precision.description: exact = a verified kickoff instant. day = a 00:00 UTC calendar-day stand-in for an unknown real kickoff time, not a verified instant.

training_same_day_proxy_rows.description: How many rows in this match's training frame are day-precision (00:00 UTC proxy) rows sharing this match's UTC calendar day. This match's own kickoff-1s cutoff cannot prove any of them actually happened first — a nonzero count means this forecast may rest on a result really played later the same day.
